# Wave H9 вҖ” Source priority + score reconciliation

**Date**: 2026-04-24
**Orchestrator**: Autonomous Fleet
**User request**:
1. Clarify "jersey numbers" framing (was misnomer for LaxNumbers team aliases).
2. Open team-score reconcile queue (29 games where player-goals sum > recorded team score).
3. Codify trust hierarchy: **PIAA > MaxPreps > PhillyLacrosse** for scores.

## Source-authority clarification (locked)

- **PIAA** publishes **season W/L/total-points** only вҖ” NO per-game scores.
- **MaxPreps** publishes per-game scores (user-provided example: `maxpreps.com/games/.../...htm`).
- **PhillyLacrosse** = current source for both per-game scores AND per-player stats.

Resulting trust rule, by data type:

| Data | Authoritative source | Fallback | Last resort |
|---|---|---|---|
| Per-game score | **MaxPreps** | PhillyLacrosse | (none) |
| Season W/L | **PIAA** | computed from games | PhillyLacrosse |
| Per-player stats | **PhillyLacrosse** | (none) | (none) |

PIAA still wins where it has data; for individual game scores it has none, so MaxPreps is the operational top of the stack for the H9 reconciliation queue.

## User decisions (orchestrator-locked, see request transcript)

- (a) MaxPreps creds: **Anon scrape first**; fall back to cookie-based auth only if anon blocked. Don't store creds in code.
- (b) Auto-apply: **Yes when MaxPreps confirms a value with high confidence** (parsed integer score вүҘ player-goals sum). Always write to `score_sources` audit table for reversibility.

**Critical path**: Lane 2 вҶ’ Lane 1 (Lane 1 stubs the fetcher import; auto-apply needs Lane 2's parser).
**Risk**: Medium (data-mutation script behind `--apply` gate; touches new migration).

### Lane 1 (Han) вҖ” boundaries
- OWN: `packages/ingest/src/migrations/013_score_sources.sql`, `packages/ingest/src/scripts/reconcileWithSources.ts`, `packages/ingest/src/scripts/__tests__/reconcileWithSources.test.ts`, `package.json` script wiring (`reconcile:scores` in `@pll/ingest`).
- DO NOT TOUCH: `sources/maxprepsGame.ts` (Lane 2), `pipelines/laxnumbers.ts`, server routes.
- Script must wire `checkServerProcs()` pgrep guard (per H6 lesson) on `--apply`.
- Schema for `score_sources`: `(id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, game_id INTEGER NOT NULL, team_side TEXT CHECK(team_side IN ('home','away')), source TEXT NOT NULL, score INTEGER NOT NULL, fetched_at TEXT NOT NULL, applied INTEGER DEFAULT 0, prior_score INTEGER, FOREIGN KEY(game_id) REFERENCES games(id))`. Index on `game_id`.
- Auto-apply rule: write to `games.home_score`/`away_score` ONLY when `mpScore >= playerGoalsSum AND mpScore <= playerGoalsSum + 5` (sanity ceiling) AND the queue entry's `currentScore < playerGoalsSum`. Else log only.

### Lane 2 (Yoda) вҖ” boundaries
- OWN: `packages/ingest/src/sources/maxprepsGame.ts`, `packages/ingest/src/sources/__tests__/maxprepsGame.test.ts`, fixture HTML under `fixtures/maxpreps/`.
- DO NOT TOUCH: `sources/maxprepsSchools.ts` (already shipped), reconcile script (Lane 1).
- Function shape: `fetchMaxprepsGameScore({ homeName, awayName, dateISO, fetchImpl?, sleepImpl? }) вҶ’ Promise<{ homeScore: number, awayScore: number, sourceUrl: string } | null>`.
- Use existing `MaxprepsSchool.maxprepsSlug` lookup logic (see `sources/maxprepsSchools.ts`) to construct URL.
- Anon User-Agent: same `'PhillyLacrosseVis/1.0 (data-aggregation; github.com/phillylacrosse)'` as laxnumbers.ts.
- Test with one captured fixture (the user's example: `4-16-2026/lacrosse-26/pottsgrove-vs-spring-ford.htm`).
- Add 1.5s polite sleep between calls. Return `null` (don't throw) on 404, login wall, or parse failure.

### Lane 3 (Leia) вҖ” boundaries
- OWN: `packages/ingest/src/scripts/piaaCheckTotals.ts`, `packages/ingest/src/scripts/__tests__/piaaCheckTotals.test.ts`.
- DO NOT TOUCH: any ingest pipeline, any migration.
- Read-only вҖ” no `--apply` flag.
- Output JSON shape: `[{ teamId, teamName, piaaWins, piaaLosses, piaaTotalPoints, computedWins, computedLosses, computedGoalsScored, deltaWins, deltaLosses, deltaPoints }]`.
- Sort by `abs(deltaWins) + abs(deltaLosses)` desc.
- Filter to teams with PIAA records present.

### Lane 4 (Chewy) вҖ” boundaries
- OWN: `docs/runbooks/source-priority.md` (NEW), edits to `.github/docs/2026-04-23-wave-h7-plan.md` + `2026-04-23-wave-h8-plan.md` (relabel "jersey numbers" вҶ’ "LaxNumbers team aliases" wherever it appears), `README.md` (add 6-line "Data sources & authority" subsection).
- DO NOT TOUCH: any code file, any other plan file.
- The runbook must include the trust-by-data-type table from this plan and a 1-paragraph note on why PIAA is empty for per-game scores.
